system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Front face (z = 0)
    ( -1   -1    0 )   // 0
    (  3   -1    0 )   // 1
    (  3    1    0 )   // 2
    ( -1    1    0 )   // 3

    // Back face (z = 0.1)
    ( -1   -1    0.1 ) // 4
    (  3   -1    0.1 ) // 5
    (  3    1    0.1 ) // 6
    ( -1    1    0.1 ) // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (120 60 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    top
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

viscosityModel  constant;

nu              1e-03;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1.5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    cylinder
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    cylinder
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalisedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

        nuInf   10;
        m       0.4;
        n       3;
    }
}')]</foamfiles>
<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.pimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword viscosityModel is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_Cmodels/runs/Cylinder/2/constant/momentumTransport/laminar"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_Cmodels/runs/Cylinder/2/constant/momentumTransport/laminar from line 13 to line 21.

    From function const Foam::entry& Foam::dictionary::lookupEntry(const Foam::word&, bool, bool) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 831.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is:

```
keyword viscosityModel is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/momentumTransport"
```

This means that when using the `generalisedNewtonian` laminar model, the solver expects a `viscosityModel` entry to be present at the same level as `laminarModel` within the `momentumTransport` dictionary. Looking at the current `momentumTransport` file, the `viscosityModel` is set to `constant`, but the `generalisedNewtonian` model requires its own internal viscosity model specification.

For the `generalisedNewtonian` model, the viscosity model is specified through the `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` sub-dictionary, where the `CrossPowerLaw` coefficients define the effective viscosity behavior. The solver needs to know which viscosity model to use, and it looks for `viscosityModel` within the context of the `generalisedNewtonian` model.

## Proposed Fix

### Modify `constant/momentumTransport`:

Remove the standalone `viscosityModel` entry and place the CrossPowerLaw coefficients directly under the `generalisedNewtonian` section. The file should look like:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalisedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        CrossPowerLawCoeffs
        {
            nuInf   10;
            m       0.4;
            n       3;
        }
    }
}
```

Alternatively, depending on the OpenFOAM version, the coefficients might need to be placed directly under `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` without the extra nesting level. The key point is that the `CrossPowerLaw` coefficients must be accessible within the `generalisedNewtonian` model context, and the solver needs to find them via the appropriate path.

Essentially, the `CrossPowerLaw` coefficients should be defined so that the `generalisedNewtonian` model can access them to compute the effective viscosity. The current file has them nested too deeply — they should either go directly into `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` or the nesting structure should match what the solver expects for the CrossPowerLaw sub-model.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'constant/momentumTransport', 'changes': 'Replace the entire content with: FoamFile { version 2.0; format ascii; class dictionary; object momentumTransport } followed by simulationType laminar; followed by laminar { laminarModel generalisedNewtonian; generalizedNewtonianCoeffs { CrossPowerLawCoeffs { nuInf 10; m 0.4; n 3; } } }'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1.5m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-03 in physical properties.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
